dialog.stepLabel: Little Girl: "They... They say you have claws......"

items.speaker: Little Girl
items.text: They... They say you have claws...

items.speaker: Little Girl
items.text: We heard you can tear those monsters into shreds...
Wanna take a look?

items.speaker: Little Boy
items.text: Yes! Please!
Okay, then! Here you go...

items.speaker: Little Girl
items.text: Eek...

items.speaker: Little Boy
items.text: C-Candies!
Try some! I hope these candies can make up for not showing you my impressive claws.

items.speaker: Little Boy
items.text: See? He's not scary at all!

items.speaker: Little Girl
items.text: Thank you, L-Lingyang!
You're welcome! Do come by to see our liondance show tomorrow!

items.speaker: Little Girl
items.text: I will!

items.speaker: Little Boy
items.text: See you soon!
Hmm... you smell unfamiliar, yet so powerful, and so pure...
You must be that famous Rover.
Hiya! I've heard a lot about you, and now I finally get to meet you in person.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: You can call me Lingyang, glad to make your acquaintance! Fare thee well, blessings swell!

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: You really are different from everyone else...

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: So we've shaken hands. Now we're good friends!

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: Hmm... So even you are scared of me, too...

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: ...Anyway! Welcome to the city of Jinzhou!

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: Our troupe is doing a big liondancing show tomorrow evening, and I'm now doing some advertising!

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: Yeah, the Liondance Troupe. We do cool liondancing shows for parades.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: Oh, and <ano=Taiping Musical>liondancing</ano> is a traditional thing in Jinzhou.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: Well, <ano=Taiping Musical>liondancing</ano> is a traditional thing in Jinzhou.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: We've got whole groups dedicated to liondancing, our troupe for example.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: We've got lots of activites in Jinzhou. I can't wait to show you!

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: (his stomach growled...)

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: Oh! Oops, uh.. Sorry, I guess I'm a bit hungry after rehearsing for a whole day.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: Speaking of that, I should treat you to dinner! Let's talk over food. That'll be fun!

options.text: That's a thing in Jinzhou too?
You seem a bit too kind...
What do you want from me?

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: Well actually... Yes it is! Jinzhou people love feeding our guests! It's our way of showing hospitality.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: Get ready to be blown away by the amazing flavors here!

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: Don't get me wrong, but this is just how we treat our guests here in Jinzhou.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: You'll love the hospitality here, I promise!

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: N-Nothing! Please believe me. I'm just trying to treat you to a meal, since you're our super important guest and all.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: You'll see how we roll with our guests soon enough!

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: But right now, I still have some other things to finish...

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: I've been getting a ton of requests for private liondances lately, and some happen to overlap with our big show tomorrow.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: And you know how important liondancing is to the folks here in Jinzhou! Can't just turn 'em down.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: So I had an idea: why not combine all these personal requests into the grand performance? That way, everyone's happy.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: We can even customize the performances to cater to each client's needs.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: But first, gotta figure out exactly what our clients want!

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: {PlayerName}, wanna come with me?

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: We can go visit my clients on our way to grab food. I'll show you around and introduce you to our local customs firsthand.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: It'll be way cooler than just hearing about it from others. What do you say?

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: Great! Let's goooo!!

dialog.stepLabel: Bei Shi: "Sorry, but I'm not the one you're looking for. I never made ..."

items.speaker: Bei Shi
items.text: Sorry, but I'm not the one you're looking for. I never made any liondancing requests...

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: {PlayerName}, what brought you here?

options.text: You didn't pay enough for the meal.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: It's not a big deal, really! Thanks for coming to check on me.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: What? Oh no! How much money did it cost you? I'll pay you back!

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: I was really freaked out for a moment. If anyone finds out I let the guest pay, I'll be shamed in Jinzhou!

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: I received a strange request from the Bei Family. It didn't mention any specific requirements.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: Could it be your parents?

items.speaker: Bei Shi
items.text: ...No, it can't be. Now, if there's nothing else, please excuse me.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: Please, one moment! Bei Shi... do you have an older brother?

items.speaker: Bei Shi
items.text: How did you know...

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: The client who called me had a mature male voice.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: And since he said he's from the Bei Family, there is only one other possibility...

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: Bei Shi, has something happened to you?

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: I'm Lingyang from the Liondance Troupe. This is {PlayerName}.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: You can trust us.

items.speaker: Bei Shi
items.text: ...It's about my brother, Bei Ji. It's been a week now, and he still hasn't come back home.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: A week ago... That's when I received this request.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: Was he acting strange the last time you saw him?

items.speaker: Bei Shi
items.text: My brother and I stopped talking to each other a while back, after a big fight. I don't even know when he last left our house...

items.speaker: Bei Shi
items.text: But... he still sends me packages from time to time. It's been a bunch of strange stuff...

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: This package has old Angelica flowers, they don't smell good anymore.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: And these meats... They are almost bad, even though they're refrigerated... Anything else he told you?

items.speaker: Bei Shi
items.text: He mentioned hanging out with people from The Savages in one of his letters...

options.text: Why didn't you inform the Patrollers?

items.speaker: Bei Shi
items.text: I didn't tell anyone, because I was worried my brother would get in trouble if I reported it to the police.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: Yeah, The Savages seem shady. And there's talk of the Jingle Beast being connected to them.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: But the return addresses on these packages are all different. How are we supposed to find him?

options.text: We can go ask the package delivery people.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: I see... {PlayerName}, you're so clever!

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: Bei Shi, leave it to me! I'll help you find your brother.

items.speaker: Bei Shi
items.text: Can I go with you?

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: I get it, Bei Shi. You really want to find your brother.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: But it's dangerous out there with the TD Outbreaks happening more frequently. I can't guarantee your safety if we run into a spreading Tacet Field.

items.speaker: Bei Shi
items.text: I... I can handle it. I used to be a member of the Pioneer Association, too.

items.speaker: Bei Shi
items.text: Us Pioneers never... never back down from a challenge...

options.text: Don't bite off more than you can chew.

items.speaker: Bei Shi
items.text: But... I...

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: If you insist... Okay, but we'll need to ask {PlayerName} about it.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: {Male=He;Female=She} is a strong Resonator. With {Male=him;Female=her}, we'll be safer on our journey.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: To be honest, I don't want to drag my friend into this... But this is an emergency, and Bei Shi want to tag along, so I'm asking a favor of you.

items.speaker: Bei Shi
items.text: {PlayerName}, I know it's a big ask...

items.speaker: Bei Shi
items.text: But please... Please help me!

options.text: Okay, then, since you're asking for my help...

items.speaker: Bei Shi
items.text: Amazing! Thank you, {PlayerName}!

items.speaker: Bei Shi
items.text: The guy who brings my packages is always the same dude, and I know where he works.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: Okay, let's get this dealt with. {PlayerName}, are you ready?

dialog.stepLabel: Stern Courier: "Phew... Thank you! You saved my life!..."

items.speaker: Stern Courier
items.text: Phew... Thank you! You saved my life!

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: Are you alright?

items.speaker: Stern Courier
items.text: Thanks for asking. I'm fine. I could have run away, but then the packages wouldn't reach their recipients...

options.text: Do people often drop off their packages here?

items.speaker: Stern Courier
items.text: Of course! "We Promise, We Deliver"! That's our slogan at the Lollo Logistics!

items.speaker: Stern Courier
items.text: All packages must be transferred at this station, but we rarely see anyone dropping off their packages here. If someone did, I will surely remember them.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: We're searching for a missing person who used your service. Can you help us?

items.speaker: Stern Courier
items.text: Sure! You just saved my life. I'll tell you everything I can remember!

options.text: Does this look familiar to you?

items.speaker: Stern Courier
items.text: Hmm, I'm sorry, but this doesn't ring any bell...

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: But we just checked the package tracker, and it said you were the deliveryman...

items.speaker: Stern Courier
items.text: ...M-Maybe it was a mistake?

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: I see... Well, we still have another question.

items.speaker: Stern Courier
items.text: The "Twinkle Beast"? You mean that monster everyone's been talking about recently?

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: You mean the "Jingle Beast", right?

items.speaker: Stern Courier
items.text: Yeah, whatever. That dangerous, man-eating thing.

items.speaker: Stern Courier
items.text: It's said to have long tusks and sharp claws that can tear anything to shreds...

items.speaker: Stern Courier
items.text: And there's this Exile group, called "The Savages," and they claim to be able to talk to that beast.

items.speaker: Stern Courier
items.text: So people have been paying them to talk the "Jingle Beast" out of attacking humans...

items.speaker: Stern Courier
items.text: "The Savages"... Yeah, sounds familiar...

items.speaker: Stern Courier
items.text: Do you know the "Tingle Beast"? That monster everyone's been talking about recently.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: You mean the "Jingle Beast", right?

items.speaker: Stern Courier
items.text: Yeah, whatever. That dangerous, man-eating thing.

items.speaker: Stern Courier
items.text: It's said that they know how to communicate with that ferocious beast.

items.speaker: Stern Courier
items.text: So people have been paying them to talk the "Jingle Beast" out of attacking humans...

items.speaker: Stern Courier
items.text: That's all I know, sir.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: Thank you, but I've got one last question.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: Do you know where we can find The Savages, or the Jingle Beast?

items.speaker: Stern Courier
items.text: ...Sorry, but we have our rules to f-follow... I cannot divulge details of our clients.

items.speaker: Stern Courier
items.text: But I heard the Jingle Beast is often found roaming in a campsite. It's somewhere near a broken tree, on that mountainside...

items.speaker: Stern Courier
items.text: Those working on the guard tower can often spot it, too.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: I see. Thank you for answering my questions.

items.speaker: Stern Courier
items.text: Sir, may I remind you...

items.speaker: Stern Courier
items.text: That thing is very dangerous... It's ferocious beyond your wildest imagination. Please be careful...

items.speaker: Stern Courier
items.text: And there may be more than one of them...

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: Don't worry! If there really was a second Jingle Beast... Well, I'll have to see it for myself!
The deliveryman thanks you and leaves...

items.speaker: Bei Shi
items.text: Oh well... We didn't get anything useful from him...

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: Yeah. At least now we know The Savages and the Jingle Beast are definitely connected.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: Besides, did you see his expression?

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: He seemed frightened. I bet he was hiding something from us out of fear.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: Well, but at least we still got some clues from him. Like where we can find the Jingle Beast.

items.speaker: Bei Shi
items.text: B-but there are so many campsites in the wild, how can we pinpoint its exact location with a couple words from him?

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: Follow me. Let's keep our eyes open for anything unusual.

items.speaker: Fierce Exile
items.text: How did it go?

items.speaker: Cautious Exile
items.text: Hehe, you're so smart, boss! That "Jingle Beast" rumor really did the trick!

items.speaker: Bei Shi
items.text: So they're really here...

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: Shh... Keep quiet. Do you see these bushes?

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: These bushes are not supposed to grow so well on exposed ground.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: Must be planted on purpose. There might be traps hiding underneath.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: Let's stay put and listen to what they're saying.

items.speaker: Cautious Exile
items.text: Oh, about that new guy. He's been getting things down quickly, I'll give him that.

items.speaker: Fierce Exile
items.text: He's too nosy. Can't shut up about the "Jingle Beast."

items.speaker: Fierce Exile
items.text: Let him go find the beast if he's so eager.

items.speaker: Fierce Exile
items.text: And then we'll handle him ourselves.

items.speaker: Cautious Exile
items.text: Boss, I heard this guy we're dealing with is tough.

items.speaker: Cautious Exile
items.text: He probably won't believe in the "Jingle Beast" rumor.

items.speaker: Fierce Exile
items.text: Let's see how he feels after we rough him up in the dark.

items.speaker: Fierce Exile
items.text: You get it now?

items.speaker: Cautious Exile
items.text: Brilliant plan, boss. You're relentless.

items.speaker: Fierce Exile
items.text: Heh, watch and learn, kid.

items.speaker: Fierce Exile
items.text: You're just a pup. You don't know what really scares people...

items.speaker: Fierce Exile
items.text: But I'll come with you this time.

items.speaker: Cautious Exile
items.text: Alright everyone, let's go! Grab your stuff!

items.speaker: Bei Shi
items.text: Phew... Are they gone?

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: No... Wait, they're still nearby.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: I can still hear them breathing. They're on high alert.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: But they're still inexperienced by beast standards.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: Okay, now they've gone farther away.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: Let's go check it out.

dialog.stepLabel: Lingyang: "{PlayerName}! Wow, it's only been minutes since I sent you t..."

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: {PlayerName}! Wow, it's only been minutes since I sent you the signal. Didn't expect you to arrive so soon!

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: While I was tailing The Savages, I overheard them talking about where Bei Ji went.

options.text: Did you hear the bell ringing?

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: A loud bell ringing? I think I heard something like that, but...

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: But I didn't... I didn't see anything like the Jingle Beast.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: Let's stick with the Bei Ji thing.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: Do you remember what we overheard at the campsite? They mentioned a newbie who was obsessed with the Jingle Beast.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: And just now, they sent him to some deep cave.

items.speaker: Bei Shi
items.text: I'm sure I did hear the bell ringing... So the Jingle Beast is probably hiding in here, too!

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: Well... Given how big it is, I don't think it could fit inside a cave like this. What do you say?

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: I do smell danger from this cave though...

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: Based on what we've gathered so far, the newbie, probably Bei Ji, is in danger. We need to act fast.

options.text: Let's go. Time is ticking down.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: ...{PlayerName}, I've always known how strong you are.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: I probably shouldn't be asking you for more help, but Bei Ji is in danger, the situation's looking dire, and we need your aid.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: Could you please help us rescue him? Your help would mean everything to me!

options.text: Sure, I'm curious what really happened.
Let's help him reunite with his family.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: Once we find him, we must make him explain. Everything!

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: Yeah. His sister is super worried too!

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: Of course! You have my word!

items.speaker: Bei Shi
items.text: I-I'm going with you!

items.speaker: Bei Shi
items.text: My brother's in there. I want to find him as soon as possible...

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: Bei Shi, I understand how much you want to find your brother.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: But it's really dark inside, and we've got no idea what could be hiding in there. We'll be stretched thin just watching each other's backs...

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: Besides, I have a more important thing for you to do.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: I need you to go back to the city as fast as you can, let Patrollers know what happened today, and request their help.

items.speaker: Bei Shi
items.text: But... I don't want to run away from danger anymore...

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: Bei Shi... As an explorer of the Pioneer Association, I trust you understand.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: A seasoned explorer knows how to make sound decisions in the moment.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: And sometimes, choosing to retreat doesn't mean we are weak.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: Please trust {PlayerName} and me. We promise to bring your brother back safely.

items.speaker: Bei Shi
items.text: ...Yes, I will. Please help him!

items.speaker: Bei Shi
items.text: And please stay safe, you two!

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: No worries. We'll bring him back!

dialog.stepLabel: Lingyang: "So, when you found out that Bei Shi lost her courage because..."

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: So, when you found out that Bei Shi lost her courage because of the Jingle Beast, you came up with a plan to get rid of it?

items.speaker: Bei Ji
items.text: Yeah... I thought I could kill the Jingle Beast, and make my sister happy again...

items.speaker: Bei Ji
items.text: And I heard rumors about an Exile group called "The Savages." Said they knew how to talk to that beast...

items.speaker: Bei Ji
items.text: So I thought...

options.text: You wanted to find the Jingle Beast through them?

items.speaker: Bei Ji
items.text: Yes... I wanted to prove my courage. Wanted to make her proud of me.

items.speaker: Bei Ji
items.text: I just wanted to show her... If her timid brother could do it, so can she!

options.text: Being brave is not the same as being reckless.

items.speaker: Bei Ji
items.text: But in the end, I couldn't achieve anything...

items.speaker: Bei Ji
items.text: Yes, you're right...

items.speaker: Bei Ji
items.text: I failed. Couldn't find the Jingle Beast, couldn't beat the bad guys. I only knew how to cower in fear... that's all I could do.

items.speaker: Bei Ji
items.text: I'm just an incompetent coward. I've always been!

items.speaker: Bei Shi
items.text: No... That's not true...

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: At least, you didn't choose to work with those bad guys.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: Succumbing to desire and killing their own kind, these are the deeds of beasts.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: It's a uniquely human trait to go against their natural instincts, even sacrificing themselves for someone else, or for a greater cause.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: It only takes a moment for a human to descend into beastliness.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: But holding on to one's humanity requires unwavering commitment and courage.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: Bei Ji, you've already proved your bravery.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: I'm sure your sister is proud of you, too.

items.speaker: Bei Ji
items.text: R-really...?

items.speaker: Bei Shi
items.text: Yes! I love you, brother. But promise me you won't do anything stupid again.

items.speaker: Bei Ji
items.text: Okay, yes, I promise you... But, um, at least I did find something this time!

items.speaker: Bei Ji
items.text: We now know the so-called Jingle Beast is just a bluff!

items.speaker: Bei Ji
items.text: So, Bei Shi, you don't need to fear it anymore!

items.speaker: Bei Shi
items.text: But I did hear the bell...

items.speaker: Bei Ji
items.text: Perhaps it was just your imagination? Or maybe it was some Tacet Discords?

items.speaker: Bei Shi
items.text: No way! I heard it loud and clear. I couldn't have mistaken it.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: Hmm... I guess we still need to help Bei Shi get over the Jingle Beast.

options.text: Why is she still so convinced?

items.speaker: Bei Ji
items.text: {PlayerName}, Lingyang, we'll be going home now. Sorry for the trouble I caused.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: Sure, go get some good rest. Don't forget to come see the liondancing show tomorrow!

items.speaker: Bei Ji
items.text: Eh? Tomorrow?

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: Don't tell me you've forgotten your liondance request?

items.speaker: Bei Ji
items.text: No, no, I didn't...

items.speaker: Bei Ji
items.text: Sorry. Actually, I made that request to further investigate the Jingle Beast...

items.speaker: Bei Ji
items.text: But now, I guess that's no longer necessary.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: I see...

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: I know you've still got questions about the Jingle Beast. Who knows? You may find the answers in tomorrow's liondancing show...

options.text: The liondancing show tomorrow is really important.

items.speaker: Bei Ji
items.text: I see. We'll come watch the show!

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: Yeah, please do come, okay?
Bei Shi and Bei Ji wave goodbye before taking their leave.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: {PlayerName}, I know you still have a lot of questions to ask...

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: And there are things I'd like to discuss with you.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: Please, follow me.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: Phew, and now we're back.

items.speaker: Qi
items.text: Lingyang! You're finally here! Master's been looking for you!

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: Oh no! I completely forgot!

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: {PlayerName}, could you look around backstage for a bit? See if you can find anything that might help Bei Shi overcome her fear...

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: I'll be right back!

items.speaker: Qi
items.text: Hurry up! Master's getting angry!

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: I'm coming! I'm coming!

dialog.stepLabel: Bei Shi: "I was once chased by a big creature in the wilderness. All I..."

items.speaker: Bei Shi
items.text: I was once chased by a big creature in the wilderness. All I could hear was a bell ringing and my only thought was to run away.

items.speaker: Bei Shi
items.text: Then I heard about the Jingle Beast, so I figured it must have been what attacked me.

items.speaker: Bei Shi
items.text: But when I saw you perform today, it all came back to me! That's the same bell ringing sound I had heard back then!

items.speaker: Bei Shi
items.text: So... I must have been saved by that bell.

items.speaker: Bei Shi
items.text: The bell that sounded like it was chasing after me... It was actually guiding me away from danger.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: The liondance inspires and empowers each person in a unique way. I'm glad my performance could give you courage and inspiration.

items.speaker: Bei Shi
items.text: Yeah! I loved your performance! I think I can keep working as Explorer for the Pioneer Association now!

items.speaker: Bei Ji
items.text: Bei Shi, that's amazing!

items.speaker: Bei Ji
items.text: When you joined the Association, I was actually so proud... I'm happy you get to keep chasing your dreams...

items.speaker: Bei Ji
items.text: And, erm, please forgive me...

items.speaker: Bei Shi
items.text: I am upset, because you never told me anything... You always carried the burden alone.

items.speaker: Bei Shi
items.text: Didn't even tell me you were gonna go kill the Jingle Beast... Don't you ever do something like that again!

items.speaker: Bei Ji
items.text: I promise, I promise. Let me treat you to something as my sincere apology.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: I know a nice food stall, it's right up ahead in the square. You should check it out.

items.speaker: Bei Ji
items.text: Awesome! Bei Shi, let's go!

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: {PlayerName}, how was my performance? Did you like it?

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: Hehe, getting your compliment is all that matters to me!

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: Hmm... Guess I still need to hone my skills a bit more...

options.text: Bravo! Bravo indeed! Such a blessing to witness.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: You're making me blush, {PlayerName}...

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: Anyway, I'm super glad to hear that from you.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: {PlayerName}, thank you so much for your help.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: I couldn't have accomplished any of this without you.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: Yeah, sure... Let's talk somewhere more private.

dialog.stepLabel: Lingyang: "Here we are. This is the place!..."

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: Here we are. This is the place!

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: Heya, mister! Lionpeppers on the beam, may your profits always stream!

items.speaker: Shuncai
items.text: Oh hi! Isn't that the Lingyang boy?

items.speaker: Shuncai
items.text: What brought you here? My liondance request? I heard you're getting a ton of requests. I hope you can still fit mine in.

items.speaker: Shuncai
items.text: Look, I got the Lionpepper and everything. Just waiting for you to put on the lion head and chow down at my store opening!

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: Sir, there's something I need to tell you...

items.speaker: Shuncai
items.text: Yeah, I heard. My thing conflicts with the performance you're doing for the soldiers, right? Will you have to cancel?

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: Exactly. So I was thinking we could incorporate some "Lionpepper" elements into the show to make up for it...

items.speaker: Shuncai
items.text: Nah, no need to worry about my little shop, boyo. Just focus on your big performance!

items.speaker: Shuncai
items.text: Oh, and you must be...

items.speaker: Shuncai
items.text: Haha, looks like Lingyang has made a new friend.

items.speaker: Shuncai
items.text: Here, try this while you're here! A new snack I just came up with!

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: Go on, {PlayerName}. Dinner's on me. Mr. Shuncai is a great chef!

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: To really get to know a city, start with the food!

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: Yum-yum! Hey sir, can we share this snack with the audience during our evening show? No snacks, no show!

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: Let me do something for you. Anything! To help you attract more customers.

items.speaker: Shuncai
items.text: Oh my, that'll be my great honor!

items.speaker: Shuncai
items.text: But...

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: What's the matter?

items.speaker: Shuncai
items.text: Have you heard? There's a monster in the wild recently, one they call the Jingle Beast...

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: The Jingle Beast...

items.speaker: Shuncai
items.text: Yeah. Somehow the nickname sounds cute even!

items.speaker: Shuncai
items.text: But you know what? It does all the terrible stuff you can think of!

items.speaker: Shuncai
items.text: When hunting, it makes these jingling sounds... They say its tusks are over half a meter long and its claws shred prey with ease.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: No, that's not true. The "Jingle Beast" doesn't look like that...

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: It... It's a... A friend of mine.

items.speaker: Shuncai
items.text: Well, actually... I need this one ingredient for my new snack, but the Jingle Beast has been messing things up. Can't find good meat anywhere right now.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: Do you have other ways to replenish your stock?

items.speaker: Shuncai
items.text: There's only one place left to get it, and it's run by this Exile group called "The Savages."

items.speaker: Shuncai
items.text: Apparently, they're in cahoots with the Jingle Beast, and that's how they still have meat for sale.

items.speaker: Shuncai
items.text: But it's crazy expensive—five times the usual price! It's insane!

items.speaker: Shuncai
items.text: I'm just a small shop, how am I supposed to afford that?

items.speaker: Shuncai
items.text: So... I can't make a lot of these snacks for you to hand out yet.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: Me? Oh... I'm fine...

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: It suddenly struck me that there was another request to deal with... It should be right next to the tea house not far away. Now, if you'll excuse me, I must go!

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: Sorry, {PlayerName}. I'll be back soon!

dialog.stepLabel: Bei Shi: "This thing may still be working.  My brother could have left..."

items.speaker: Bei Shi
items.text: This thing may still be working.  My brother could have left me a message...

items.speaker: Bei Shi
items.text: Hmm... Ah! There is a message!

items.speaker: Bei Ji (Hologram)
items.text: Sorry Bei Shi... I stood between you and your dreams.

items.speaker: Bei Ji (Hologram)
items.text: Perhaps you were right... Your brother is just a selfish coward.

items.speaker: Bei Ji (Hologram)
items.text: I don't dream of your forgiveness... But still...  I want to do something for you.

items.speaker: Bei Ji (Hologram)
items.text: Farewell, Bei Shi. I'll kill the Jingle Beast... for you...

items.speaker: Bei Ji (Hologram)
items.text: (The video ends here...)

items.speaker: Bei Shi
items.text: I-It's my brother...

items.speaker: Bei Shi
items.text: It's probably because I joined the Pioneer Association.

items.speaker: Bei Shi
items.text: My brother was really against my decision...

items.speaker: Bei Shi
items.text: But that was my childhood dream, so we had a big fight...

options.text: Why is he trying to kill the "Jingle Beast"?

items.speaker: Bei Shi
items.text: I... I don't know. He never told me anything about it...

items.speaker: Unknown (83)
items.text: (Ring... Ring...)

items.speaker: Bei Shi
items.text: Eek!

items.speaker: Bei Shi
items.text: No... It must be the Jingle Beast!

items.speaker: Bei Shi
items.text: I know... I know that sound too well.

items.speaker: Bei Shi
items.text: A while ago, when I was on a mission in the wilderness, I got ambushed by Tacet Discords...

items.speaker: Bei Shi
items.text: As I struggled to escape, I heard the sound of a bell ringing in the distance...

items.speaker: Bei Shi
items.text: I-I can't remember... I was so scared, my mind went completely blank.

items.speaker: Bei Shi
items.text: After hearing the bell, I...

items.speaker: Bei Shi
items.text: I just kept running without looking back. That bell kept chasing me and only disappeared when I reached a safer place.

items.speaker: Bei Shi
items.text: If I hadn't made it, perhaps I would have been eaten by the Jingle Beast...

items.speaker: Bei Shi
items.text: So... Yes, this must be the Jingle Beast again!

items.speaker: Bei Shi
items.text: Alright... L-Let's go...

dialog.stepLabel: Bei Shi: "This is... This is my brother's device...."

items.speaker: Bei Shi
items.text: This is... This is my brother's device.

items.speaker: Bei Shi
items.text: How could this be... Is my brother really working for those bad guys?

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: Bei Shi, what kind of person is he?

items.speaker: Bei Shi
items.text: He's shy and careful, but not sneaky. He may be timid, but he really loves me...

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: Bei Shi... Sometimes, we can't trust what we hear or see.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: We have to use our hearts and logic to uncover the real truth, it's not always obvious.

items.speaker: Bei Shi
items.text: ...Thank you. I see.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: {PlayerName}, you found anything else?

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: It's just the tusk of an adult Sabyr Boar. It's indeed larger than usual, but...

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: They must be using this to make people believe the "Jingle Beast" is real.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: It's been refrigerated for a while, but the inside has spoiled... Exactly like the thing we found in one of those packages...

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: I can even smell the chemical preservatives in there...

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: They're not just spreading rumors, they are also selling spoiled meat.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: Ugh... It pisses me off, too.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: The top priority now is to find clues about Bei Ji's whereabouts.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: Now we have another problem. We have to keep tabs on "The Savages" to see what they're planning.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: According to what we just heard, their next victim is about to show up.

options.text: I'll go look for Bei Ji.
I'll go look for The Savages.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: Sounds good. The Savages are super alert, but I know the wilderness better than them. I can track them down.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: {PlayerName}, I understand your anger.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: But they are super alert. It'll be too risky for you to go alone. Besides, I know the wilderness better than them. Maybe I should go.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: I want to ask you to take Bei Shi and go look for her brother.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: She'll be safer with you by her side.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: If anything happens, I'll "call" you.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: Thank you, {PlayerName}. You take care of yourself too.

dialog.stepLabel: Cautious Exile: "Why... Why did this happen?..."

items.speaker: Cautious Exile
items.text: Why... Why did this happen?

items.speaker: Fierce Exile
items.text: When it rains, it pours...

items.speaker: Fierce Exile
items.text: This is not what I had planned!

options.text: You can't always get what you want.

items.speaker: Serious Patroller
items.text: Behave!

items.speaker: Bei Shi
items.text: Lingyang! Rover!

items.speaker: Upright Patroller
items.text: Thanks for the tip, young lady. We made it in time to catch them because of you.

items.speaker: Bei Shi
items.text: Lingyang! {PlayerName}! Are you alright?

items.speaker: Bei Ji
items.text: Bei Shi...

items.speaker: Bei Shi
items.text: Bei Ji!

items.speaker: Bei Ji
items.text: Bei Shi, I'm so sorry I made you worry...

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: Though seeing your reunion is truly touching...

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: I still gotta say, this isn't the right place for conversation. Let's head back to the city first.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: {PlayerName}, let's talk here.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: Where should I begin...

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: {PlayerName}, have you ever thought about this...

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: Since there are humans with Resonance Abilities...

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: ...Then, could there ever be beasts with the same kind of power?

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: Uncontrolled emotions in human Resonators lead to <ano=Overclocking>disasters</ano>. It's even more dangerous in beasts, as they follow instincts.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: Over time, most of them died out, driven extinct by their unrestrained desires.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: Beasts that can bring their power under control are extremely rare, but... they do exist.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: And the Jingle Beast... It happens to be one of them.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: A long, long time ago, the Jingle Beast wasn't called the Jingle Beast. It used to have a name—

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: It was called "Suan'ni."

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: Back then, the Suan'nis lived a tranquil life in deep forests, far away from humans.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: However, when the Tacet Fields expanded rapidly, their territories were not spared.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: Some of them had to encroach upon certain human territories for a chance to survive.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: Humans suffered greatly against the Suan'nis, with their terrifying appearance, powerful Resonance Abilities, and wild animal instincts...

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: ...And that's why the Suan'nis were deemed evil.

options.text: How do you know all that?

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: Uh... I read it in an old book in Huanglong. Said they lived under that giant Violet Banyan tree in the Dim Forest.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: The Suan'nis have nearly gone extinct over the years. And now, there might be only one of them remaining.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: And... I've heard of an interesting theory from the archaeologists...

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: That last Suan'ni might have yearned to become a human.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: To make itself more approachable, it first came up with a less intimidating name... Something like, the "Jingle Beast."

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: And then, it yanked off all its fur, filed down its claws, twisted its bones, and learned to stand upright...

options.text: Why did it want to become human so bad?

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: Maybe... It was drawn by the bustle and merriment of human cities. It must have been lonely as the last of its kind.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: Maybe it was moved by human bravery against the Lament, and wanted to offer help. Maybe it wanted to fight the catastrophe that ruined its once homeland.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: Or maybe, it simply couldn't resist human foods?

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: Anyway... It never really became a human in the end.

options.text: Is it because of its appearance?

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: If it truly wanted to become human, it had to first understand the essence of what makes a human.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: If you ask me, the essence would be the 'heart' of a human.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: Therefore, the "Jingle Beast" no longer rejected its appearance and began to, in its animal form, connect with humans, to assist them...

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: Perhaps, in this process, it happened to save a girl in the wilderness.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: {PlayerName}, what do you think the ending for the "Jingle Beast" was? Did it finally become a human?

options.text: It has a long way to go.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: Well... is that what you think?

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: I think the "Jingle Beast" no longer cares whether it's a human or a beast. It lives in its own way.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: In this world, there are beings with the appearance of a beast and the soul of a human, just as there are beings with the appearance of a human and the soul of a beast. All it needs to do is be true to itself.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: It will remain resolute and convey the beautiful and inspiring qualities it once saw in humans in its own way.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: Oops, I got carried away and almost forgot—here, keep this bell as a memento!

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: Thank you, {PlayerName}!
Lingyang! Come here!

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: Coming!

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: {PlayerName}, I must go get the props ready for the next show.

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: Come watch my performance again next time! Food's on me!

items.speaker: Lingyang
items.text: Please keep this bell! It should bring you good luck! Fare thee well, blessings swell!
